planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Runbook and workbook include both +5% and -5% perturbation tables; reviewer can see per-record priority and class before and after each perturbation; summary indicates whether expert_review flag triggers.
planned_changes.description: Add -5% perturbation runs for every numeric input used in classification (weights and other numeric drivers). For each perturbation, compute priorities, classes, and present a per-record table showing any class flips.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Artifact previews (plain-text CSV sections or formula map) are present in runbook_change.md and compaction_roi.xlsx contains matching cells; verifier can validate row-level calculations without openpyxl.
planned_changes.description: Embed CSV exports or textual sheet dumps for every worksheet with formulas and add a 'Formula Map' table that lists key cell addresses, formula expressions, and source citations.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Runbook documents cell addresses and reviewer closure conditions; 'needs_review' includes a checklist of required artifact/data to resolve ambiguity.
planned_changes.description: Annotate the workbook with explicit cell references for min/max normalization, weights, threshold, and scenario cost computations. In runbook_change.md, specify what evidence or packet updates would allow clearing 'needs_review'.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Runbook includes validation note and at least one sample cell formula and its expected evaluated value.
planned_changes.description: Add a short validation note describing how the OpenXML-assembled .xlsx was validated (manual spot checks, opening in Excel) and include a small checksum or sample formula text to prove fidelity.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: New verifier/grader evidenceRefs point to the revised artifacts and verifier status improves to 'verified' with programmatic parsing OK or with embedded CSV proof.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Deliverables are present and reproducible; deterministic precheck and verifier accepted the artifact contract. Two actionable gaps were identified: (1) programmatic parsing of the .xlsx was blocked in the grader environment (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-level formula/citation verification could not be independently validated; (2) sensitivity testing documented only +5% single-weight perturbations rather than both +5% and -5% as required by the rubric. Worker correctly documented assumptions (equal weights) and flagged 'needs_review' where packet details were missing.
